app.name: Open DroneLog
app.one_liner: A high-performance, local-first drone flight log analyzer supporting DJI and Litchi formats with rich telemetry visualizations and offline-first privacy.

screens.name: Application Settings
screens.purpose: Configure date format, profiles, password protection, and manage log storage with clear options for deletion or re-import.

screens.name: Battery Health Report
screens.purpose: Show battery capacity over time, lifetime degradation trends, and flight count per battery with full/remaining capacity tracking.

screens.name: Flight Overview Dashboard
screens.purpose: Display aggregated flight statistics, radial hour-of-flight chart, and list of imported flights with quick access to telemetry and map views.
primary_actions: Filter flights by drone or battery

screens.name: Interactive Flight Map
screens.purpose: Render 3D terrain flight paths with satellite/topographic/OpenStreetMap layers, speed-controlled replay, and live telemetry overlay.
primary_actions: View RC joystick input during flight

screens.name: Telemetry Charts
screens.purpose: Visualize synchronized time-series data including height, speed, battery voltage, attitude, GPS, and RC signals with drag-to-zoom functionality.

rust_commands.name: extract_battery_capacity
rust_commands.purpose: Extract battery full and remaining capacity from flight logs during parsing for use in telemetry and degradation reports.

rust_commands.name: import_flight_logs
rust_commands.purpose: Parse and ingest DJI (.txt) or Litchi CSV logs into the local DuckDB database with deduplication based on drone serial, battery serial, and start time.

rust_commands.name: preserve_record_customization
rust_commands.purpose: Restore user-added notes, tags, colors, and names when re-importing logs with identical file hash.

rust_commands.name: render_radial_flight_hour_chart
rust_commands.purpose: Generate a radial chart showing distribution of flight start times across hours of the day, localized to flight origin timezone.

rust_commands.name: tag_drone_decommissioned
rust_commands.purpose: Mark a drone as decommissioned by detecting '[X]' in its custom name; hides it from maintenance views and grays it out in UI.

mvp_plan: week 1: Build main dashboard screen with flight list
week 1: Create flights table with deduplication logic
week 1: Implement basic DJI .txt log parser
week 1: Set up Tauri v2 + React + DuckDB scaffold
week 2: Add macOS packaging workaround documentation
week 2: Add telemetry_samples ingestion and downsampling
week 2: Integrate OpenStreetMap flight path rendering
week 3: Add decommission tagging for drones/batteries
week 3: Develop battery health report with capacity tracking
week 3: Enable record customization persistence on re-import
week 4: Add profile management and password protection
week 4: Address stack overflow risk in log ingestion pipeline
week 4: Write acceptance tests for bulk import and deduplication
acceptance_tests: Given 100+ DJI logs, when importing, then app does not crash with 'maximum call stack size exceeded' error.
Given a drone labeled '[X] Retired', when viewing the maintenance section, then the drone is hidden and grayed out in all lists.
Given a flight log with known battery capacity values, when re-imported after deletion, then user-added notes and color tags are restored if file hash matches.
Given a macOS user following the installation guide, when launching the app, then it opens without 'Damaged File' or 'Can't open file' errors.
Given multiple flights across timezones, when viewing the radial hour chart, then flight start times are correctly binned by local hour of origin location.
